name: Chicken Loaves with Marsala Mushroom Sauce
recipeInstructions:
For the sauce:
6 garlic cloves - coarsely chopped
1/4 cup olive oil
1 cup sliced mushrooms
1 cup Marsala wine
1 cup low sodium chicken broth
2 tablespoons unsalted butter
1. In a saucepan, cook onion in oil over moderate heat, stirring occasionally until softened and cooled.
2. In a large bowl, combine well the ground chicken, celery, parsley, garlic, eggs, milk, Parmesan cheese, and bread crumbs. Mix in the cooled onion mixture. Cover and chill for 1 hour.
3. Divide the chicken mixture into 5 equal portions and form into small loaves.
4. In a large deep skillet, cook garlic in oil over moderate heat until golden. Remove and discard garlic. Add chicken loaves and cook, turning once, until golden.
5. Add mushrooms and Marsala wine to the skillet and cook until the liquid is reduced by half. Add chicken broth and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until a meat thermometer inserted in the center registers 170°F.
6. Transfer loaves to a platter. Reduce sauce by half, whisk in butter, and pour sauce over the loaves. Serves 5 generously.
